revision.comment: Bot: mark 3 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:縣神社 (下呂市)]]
{{Unreferenced|date=March 10, 2015 (Tuesday) 13:39 (UTC)|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Agata Shrine |image=File:Agata jinja hagiwara.jpg |location = 311 Kanda, Sakurabuchi, Hagiwara-chō, Gero City, Gifu Prefecture | Latitude Degrees =35 |Latitude Minutes = 52|Latitude Seconds =58.9 | Longitude Degrees=137 |Longitude Minutes =13 |Longitude Seconds =18.5 |deity = Ootoshikami |type = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |festival = April |prefecture= {{ill|Gero|qid=Q819639|1=Gero, Gifu}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.88302777777778|N|137.22180555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Agata Shrine''' (縣神社, Agata Jinja) is an {{ill|independent Shinto shrine|ja|単立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/independent Shinto shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/independent Shinto shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 単立|redirects to 単立|1=Independent Shinto shrine|2=ja|3=単立|1=Independent Shinto shrine|2=ja|3=単立|redirects to 単立|1=Independent Shinto shrine|2=ja|3=単立|}} located in {{ill|Hagiwara-chō|ja|萩原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagiwara-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagiwara-chō|1=Hagiwara-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620353}}, {{ill|Gero, Gifu|en|Gero, Gifu|qid=Q819639|lt=Gero City|1=Gero, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. Its former shrine Rank was a village shrine. == Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Toshigami|ja|御歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} * Enshrined Deities: {{ill|Ietsumiko no Kami|ja|家津御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsumiko no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ietsumiko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500709}} (also known as Ietsumiko no Kami), {{ill|Kumano Kusu no Mi no Mikoto|ja|熊野久須美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusu no Mi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusu no Mi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500713}} (also known as Kumano Musubi no Kami), {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} == History == The sacred object is a naturally shaped stone resembling a mountain. The shrine is situated nearly in the center of the rice field area of the Sakurabuchi alluvial fan. According to the shrine’s historical records, "Since the beginning of land development, the villagers designated this central site as a place for ceremonial plowing and rope laying, eventually enshrining Ootoshikami, the guardian deity of rice fields and grains." The area surrounding the shrine is the {{ill|Jinden Site|ja|神田遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502222|1=Kanda Site}}, a Jomon-period archaeological site where numerous stone tools and Jomon pottery have been excavated. During an excavation in 1973 (Showa 48), dwelling remains and human bones were also discovered. == Timeline == * Founded: Unknown * 1690 (Genroku 3): Rebuilt * 1872 (Meiji 5): Designated as a village shrine * 1908 (Meiji 41): Enshrined four other shrines within the Sakurabuchi area—Kumano Shrine, Tenno Shrine, and two Yama Shrines. * 1952 (Showa 27): Became a religious corporation as Agata Shrine. == Cultural Properties == * Gifu Prefecture Designated Natural Monument: {{ill|Cedar of Agata Shrine|ja|県神社のスギ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cedar of Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cedar of Agata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500718}}<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/gerosi/agatajinnjya.html |title=Cedar of Agata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=October 16, 2012|qq=}}</ref> == References == {{Reference Help|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * *Hagiwara Town History*, Hagiwara Town, 1962 * *Hagiwara Town Historical Records*, Hagiwara Town, 2002 * *Hida Sakurabuchi & Okita*, Hagiwara Town Board of Education, 1974 {{citation needed|ja|縣神社|version=101733825|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|
[[ja:縣神社 (下呂市)]] }}
[[ja:縣神社]] [[ja:縣神社 (下呂市)]]
